article_title: Unitree Debuts the GD01, a Giant Transformable Mecha Robot That Carries Human Pilots and Switches Between Bipedal and Quadrupedal Modes
summary: Auto-verdict was APPROVE; manual review found two compound source-attribution errors where quotes from The Verge (source-1) are cited as if from WIRED (source-0). File corrections; merge.

findings.message: 'Mechanical horse' phrasing attributed to WIRED but only appears in The Verge
findings.details: Article paragraph: 'The transformation allows the machine to operate in a four-legged "mechanical horse" or crabwalk-like stance, in which a pilot would be positioned on their back, according to [WIRED](...)' — the 'mechanical horse' framing is in The Verge (source-1: 'carrying it around on four legs like an unsettling mechanical horse'); the WIRED snapshot uses 'crawling on its hands and legs' and 'crabwalk position'. The 'crabwalk' + 'lying on their back' attribution to WIRED is correct, but 'mechanical horse' should be attributed to The Verge.

findings.message: Safety quotes ('dangerous modifications', 'hazardous manner') attributed to WIRED + The Verge but only in The Verge
findings.details: The quotes 'refrain from making any dangerous modifications' and 'using the robot in a hazardous manner' are present only in The Verge snapshot (source-1). The WIRED snapshot (source-0) contains 'Please everyone be sure to use the robot in a Friendly and Safe manner' but not the 'dangerous modifications' / 'hazardous manner' wording. Source-2 (Interesting Engineering) paraphrases as 'hazardous modifications or extreme tests'.

Article says 'WIRED and The Verge both verifying directly with the company'. WIRED did verify directly. The Verge (source-1) writes 'Wired confirmed with Unitree' — i.e., The Verge cites WIRED's verification rather than independently verifying. Minor framing slip.

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate — every quantitative claim (1.6x height, 500 kg / 1,102 lb, $650,000 price, 5,500 humanoid robots shipped 2025, ~90% Chinese share of humanoid sales) and every quoted material is verifiable in at least one cited snapshot. CEO name (Xingxing Wang / Wang Xingxing — same person, different name-order conventions across sources), Hangzhou base, STAR Market IPO filing, Omdia + SCMP attribution, comparator company list (Tesla, Figure AI, Agility Robotics) all verified. Two compound source-attribution errors: the 'mechanical horse' framing and the safety-disclaimer quotes ('dangerous modifications', 'hazardous manner') are attributed to WIRED in the article but appear only in The Verge. File a corrections record and merge.

source-0.status: WIRED. Verifies: $650,000 price, 'Friendly and Safe' quote, 'crabwalk position', pilot 'lying on their back', Xingxing Wang CEO + Hangzhou base, 'product it is selling, not an elaborate prank' direct verification. Does NOT contain 'mechanical horse' or 'dangerous modifications / hazardous manner' quotes — those are in The Verge.

source-1.status: The Verge. Verifies: $650,000 price, 'It can transform. It's a civilian vehicle. It weighs ~500kg with you inside' quote verbatim, 'mechanical horse' phrasing, 'dangerous modifications' + 'hazardous manner' quotes, Wang Xingxing in cockpit, bipedal↔quadrupedal switching, 'isn't a prank (Wired confirmed with Unitree)'.

source-2.status: Interesting Engineering (citing 36Kr, Omdia, SCMP). Verifies: 1.6× height of average adult, 500 kg / 1102 lb with pilot, high-strength alloy, stack-of-bricks demo, leg-folding + chassis transformation, 5,500 humanoid robots shipped by Unitree in 2025, ~90% Chinese global humanoid share, Tesla/Figure AI/Agility Robotics shipping ~150 units each, STAR Market IPO in March (article writes 'March 2026' which is inferred but consistent with the May 2026 article date). Also contains $650,000 in title and 3.9M yuan (US$573,674) as the official starting price.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well structured (Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis). Neutral tone, appropriate skepticism toward marketing-style demos. Length appropriate for News (768 words).
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of the Unitree GD01 or any manned mecha robot.
concerns: Compound source attribution: 'mechanical horse' framing and the 'dangerous modifications / hazardous manner' safety quotes are attributed to WIRED but appear only in The Verge. The article does cite both outlets but readers cannot tell which outlet a given phrase came from.
recommendations: Bot guidance: when a quote appears in only one of multiple cited outlets, attribute the quote to that specific outlet rather than to a compound '[A] and [B]' link.

content_preview.title: Unitree Debuts the GD01, a Giant Transformable Mecha Robot That Carries Human Pilots and Switches Between Bipedal and Quadrupedal Modes
content_preview.summary: Unitree unveils the GD01, a large manned mecha that transforms between bipedal and quadrupedal modes, carries pilots in a torso cockpit, and is priced around $650,000.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Chinese robotics company Unitree has unveiled the GD01, a large mecha-style robot standing roughly 1.6 times the height of an average adult that can switch between bipedal and quadrupedal modes, carries a human pilot in a torso-mounted cockpit, and is priced at around $650,000, according to [The Verge](https://www.theverge.com/tech/929487/unitree-gd01-giant-mech-suit-robot) and [Interesting Engineering](https://interestingengineering.com/ai-robotics/video-unitree-launches-the-worlds...
